name: Cedar Creek Community Center
description: Cedar Creek Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can’t reserve the courts. The courts are private.
fees: Private — There is a non member fee.

name: East Aiken School of the Arts
description: These courts are only for the students of East Aiken School of the Arts.

name: Eustis Park
description: Come play pickleball at Eustis Park in Aiken, SC! There are 5 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Gem Lakes Recreation Association
description: Gem Lakes Recreation Association is a place to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Reservations are not available. The courts are private.
open_play_schedule: Private courts but available to play with residents of Gem Lakes
fees: Private — None if playing with resident of Gem Lakes

name: Gregg Park
description: Gregg Park is a place to play pickleball in Graniteville, SC. There are 2 outdoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Amenities include restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Hammond Hills Suburban Club
description: Hammond Hills Suburban Club is a place to play pickleball in North Augusta, SC. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. The courts can be reserved. Players have access to restrooms, water, lights, and trainers or lessons.
fees: Members only — Family and Individual Membership along with monthly memberships available.

name: Harrison-Caver Park
description: Come play pickleball at Harrison-Caver Park in Warrenville, SC! There are 4 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to lights and wheelchair accessibility.
open_play_schedule: Courts are open every day from dawn to dusk.

name: Houndslake Country Club
description: Houndslake Country Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights.

name: New Ellenton STEAM Middle School
description: These courts are for students at New Ellenton STEAM Middle School only.

name: Odell Weeks Activity Center
description:
Lessons and Clinics available. 
Head Pro: Mo Garcia 
Asst. Pro: Harve Mobley

name: Smith-Hazel Park
description: Smith-Hazel Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Tall Pine STEM Academy
description: Tall Pine STEM Academy is a place to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 3 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility. The courts are private.

name: The Reserve Club At Woodside Plantation
description: The Reserve Club At Woodside Plantation is a place to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms and water.

name: Virginia Acres Park
description:
Free Parking, walking track, Access to 9 indoors courts (Odell Weeks Activity Center.) 
Practice Wall available.
Access to multiple food & drink shops and restaurants.
Nearby Hotels and shopping.
1.5 miles from historical Downtown Aiken. 
Open Play and league play available.

name: Woodside Plantation Country Club
description: Woodside Plantation Country Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Aiken, SC. There are 4 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play.
